quickFacts: The Louvre is free on the first Sunday of each month
Métro tickets are cheaper in a carnet of 10
Skip the Eiffel Tower queue — book online weeks ahead
Tap water is free at restaurants — ask for 'une carafe d'eau'
foodScene: Croissants from a real boulangerie are life-changing. Hit Marché des Enfants Rouges for global street food. Crêpes in Montparnasse, not at tourist stands. Duck confit at a classic bistro. End the night with natural wine at a cave à manger.

places.name: Eiffel Tower
places.caption: The icon of icons
places.description: Go at sunset for golden hour magic. The second floor has the best views-to-queue ratio. Picnic on the Champ de Mars with wine and cheese.

places.name: Montmartre & Sacré-Cœur
places.caption: Bohemian hilltop village
places.description: Wander cobblestone streets, discover hidden vineyards, and catch panoramic views from the basilica steps. Skip the portrait artists, explore the backstreets.

places.name: Le Marais
places.caption: Trendy historic quarter
places.description: The best falafel at L'As du Fallafel, vintage shopping, and gorgeous Place des Vosges. Equal parts medieval charm and modern cool.

places.name: Musée d'Orsay
places.caption: Impressionist paradise
places.description: Monet, Renoir, and Van Gogh in a stunning Beaux-Arts train station. Less crowded than the Louvre and arguably better art for most visitors.

places.name: Canal Saint-Martin
places.caption: Local hangout vibes
places.description: Iron footbridges, tree-lined banks, and the best people-watching in Paris. Grab a baguette sandwich and sit by the locks.

places.name: Père Lachaise Cemetery
places.caption: Hauntingly beautiful
places.description: Jim Morrison, Oscar Wilde, Chopin — this gothic garden cemetery is equal parts history walk and peaceful green escape.

places.name: Shakespeare & Company
places.caption: Legendary bookshop
places.description: A living literary landmark since the 1950s. Browse rare editions, sit in the reading nook, then grab coffee at the café next door with Notre-Dame views.

places.name: Sainte-Chapelle
places.caption: Stained glass masterpiece
places.description: The upper chapel's floor-to-ceiling 13th-century windows are jaw-dropping. Shorter lines and cheaper than most attractions.

places.name: Rue Crémieux
places.caption: Pastel-coloured street
places.description: The most Instagrammed street in Paris with candy-coloured facades. Quick photo stop, but please respect the residents.

places.name: Day Trip: Versailles
places.caption: Palace & gardens
places.description: The Hall of Mirrors and Marie Antoinette's hamlet are worth the 40-minute train ride. Go early, start with the gardens to dodge the palace crowds.

places.name: Louvre Museum
places.caption: World's largest museum
places.description: 380,000 objects including the Mona Lisa and Venus de Milo. Don't try to see everything — pick a wing and go deep.

places.name: Sacré-Cœur & Montmartre
places.caption: Hilltop basilica
places.description: White-domed basilica overlooking Paris from Montmartre hill. Artists at Place du Tertre, vineyard, Moulin Rouge nearby.

places.name: Jardin du Luxembourg
places.caption: Royal gardens
places.description: Formal French gardens with an octagonal pond, orchards, free chairs, puppet theatre and pétanque courts. Quintessentially Parisian.

places.name: Notre-Dame Cathedral
places.caption: Reopened after the fire
places.description: Gothic masterpiece rebuilt after the 2019 fire. The new golden spire, cleaned interior and medieval rose windows are breathtaking.

places.name: Seine River Cruise
places.caption: Floating tour
places.description: Pass every major monument from the water. Evening dinner cruises or budget Batobus hop-on boats. Pont Alexandre III is stunning.
